{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 1","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","new":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            *[ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM) for _ in range(DEPTH)],
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The optimizer/schedule side is already improved; the remaining headroom is in model capacity. This edit turns the dead DEPTH constant into real residual depth and widens the network modestly, which is the safest next architecture move under the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["summaries","indexing","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_function","function":"build_scheduler","old":"","new":"","anchor":"","text":"","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    warmup_steps = max(1, total_steps // 20)
    cosine_steps = max(1, total_steps - warmup_steps)
    warmup = optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(optimizer, start_factor=0.2, total_iters=warmup_steps)
    cosine = optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=cosine_steps)
    return optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR(optimizer, [warmup, cosine], milestones=[warmup_steps])"}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The model is now meaningfully deeper, so a short warmup should make the early optimization phase smoother before cosine decay takes over. This is a low-risk schedule refinement that targets the remaining instability in fixed-step training.","secondary_modes":["indexing","topk","layout","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 3","new":"DEPTH = 2","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"class ResidualBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, channels: int, dropout: float = 0.0, use_bn: bool = True):
        super().__init__()
        bias = not use_bn
        layers1: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        layers2: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if use_bn:
            layers1.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
            layers2.insert(1, nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        self.conv1 = nn.Sequential(*layers1)
        self.conv2 = nn.Sequential(*layers2)
        self.activation = nn.ReLU(inplace=True)
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout2d(dropout) if dropout > 0 else nn.Identity()

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        residual = x
        out = self.activation(self.conv1(x))
        out = self.dropout(out)
        out = self.conv2(out)
        out = self.activation(out + residual)
        return out","new":"class ResidualBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self,
        in_channels: int,
        out_channels: int | None = None,
        stride: int = 1,
        dropout: float = 0.0,
        use_bn: bool = True,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        out_channels = in_channels if out_channels is None else out_channels
        bias = not use_bn
        self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(in_channels, out_channels, 3, stride=stride, padding=1, bias=bias)
        self.bn1 = nn.BatchNorm2d(out_channels) if use_bn else nn.Identity()
        self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(out_channels, out_channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)
        self.bn2 = nn.BatchNorm2d(out_channels) if use_bn else nn.Identity()
        if stride != 1 or in_channels != out_channels:
            skip_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(in_channels, out_channels, 1, stride=stride, bias=bias)]
            if use_bn:
                skip_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(out_channels))
            self.skip = nn.Sequential(*skip_layers)
        else:
            self.skip = nn.Identity()
        self.activation = nn.ReLU(inplace=True)
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout2d(dropout) if dropout > 0 else nn.Identity()

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        residual = self.skip(x)
        out = self.activation(self.bn1(self.conv1(x)))
        out = self.dropout(out)
        out = self.bn2(self.conv2(out))
        out = self.activation(out + residual)
        return out","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        channels = BASE_CHANNELS
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM
        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(channels))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)
        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            *[ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM) for _ in range(DEPTH)],
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(channels, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.blocks(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","new":"class CIFAR10Net(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        super().__init__()
        c1 = BASE_CHANNELS
        c2 = BASE_CHANNELS * CHANNEL_MULT
        c3 = c2 * CHANNEL_MULT
        bias = not USE_BATCHNORM

        stem_layers: list[nn.Module] = [nn.Conv2d(INPUT_CHANNELS, c1, 3, padding=1, bias=bias)]
        if USE_BATCHNORM:
            stem_layers.append(nn.BatchNorm2d(c1))
        stem_layers.append(nn.ReLU(inplace=True))
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(*stem_layers)

        self.stage1 = nn.Sequential(
            *[ResidualBlock(c1, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM) for _ in range(DEPTH)]
        )
        self.down1 = ResidualBlock(c1, c2, stride=2, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM)
        self.stage2 = nn.Sequential(
            *[ResidualBlock(c2, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM) for _ in range(DEPTH)]
        )
        self.down2 = ResidualBlock(c2, c3, stride=2, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM)
        self.stage3 = nn.Sequential(
            *[ResidualBlock(c3, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM) for _ in range(DEPTH)]
        )
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((1, 1))
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(c3, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.stage1(x)
        x = self.down1(x)
        x = self.stage2(x)
        x = self.down2(x)
        x = self.stage3(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The warmup scheduler did not help, so the next plausible gain is to spend the budget on a proper stagewise residual hierarchy instead of more schedule tuning. This change adds projection shortcuts and downsampling, which should improve feature quality more than another scalar tweak.","secondary_modes":["summaries","indexing","micro"]}
